sections.paragraph:
ὑπομονητικός, ή, όν (ὑπομονή; Hippocr.+; Philo, Leg. All. 3, 88) pert. to being able to hold up in the face of difficulty, patient, showing endurance neut. subst. τὸ ὑπομονητικόν fortitude MPol 2:2.—DELG s.v. μένω.

Hippocr Hippocr = Corpus Hippocraticum, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

neut. neut. = neuter (used when “n.” would be ambiguous)

subst. subst. = substantive(ly)

MPol MPol = Martyrdom of Polycarp; after II a.d.—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1040
